item.title: 13 year old aspiring referee's dream comes true at Eden Park
item.description: Vaibhavi, who hopes to one day officiate at the highest level of world rugby, was in Auckland with her father, Dhanjay Deo, to watch the All Blacks take on Ireland when she received an unexpected surprise. Just before kick-off, she was invited from her seat to the sideline, where she joined the international match officials as they completed their final preparations for the Test.
item.content:encoded:
Vaibhavi, who hopes to one day officiate at the highest level of world rugby, was in Auckland with her father, Dhanjay Deo, to watch the All Blacks take on Ireland when she received an unexpected surprise.

Just before kick-off, she was invited from her seat to the sideline, where she joined the international match officials as they completed their final preparations for the Test.

From ground level, Vaibhavi watched the teams run onto the field, witnessed the national anthems and the famous All Blacks haka, and spent the match learning how elite referees prepare, communicate and work together.

Speaking after the experience, Vaibhavi says she had no idea what was happening when she was taken from her seat and described it as the biggest surprise of her life.

She says seeing how international referees prepare for a Test match and watching the haka from the sideline, has inspired her to work even harder towards her goal of one day returning to Eden Park as a referee.

After the match, Australian referee Nic Berry presented Vaibhavi with a signed World Rugby match official's jersey, to encourage her to continue pursuing her dream.

Rugby Australia has also expressed their interest in supporting her development as she works towards becoming an international referee.

Her father, Dhanjay Deo, says the trip was originally planned so they could watch their first All Blacks Test together, but the surprise made it an experience the family will never forget.

Vaibhavi's journey has also been supported by Fiji Airways, while the Fiji Rugby Union and the Suva Rugby Referees Association continue to mentor and encourage her as she pursues her dream of becoming a top-level rugby referee.

item.title: England beat France 6-4 in thrilling World Cup third-place play-off
item.description: England survived a fierce second-half comeback from France to claim third place at the FIFA World Cup with a thrilling 6-4 victory. After storming to a 4-0 halftime lead, England were pushed all the way as Kylian Mbappé scored twice to become the FIFA World Cup's all-time leading goalscorer and move to the top of the Golden Boot race.
item.content:encoded:
England survived a fierce second-half comeback from France to claim third place at the FIFA World Cup with a thrilling 6-4 victory.

After storming to a 4-0 halftime lead, England were pushed all the way as Kylian Mbappé scored twice to become the FIFA World Cup's all-time leading goalscorer and move to the top of the Golden Boot race.

Declan Rice got England off to the perfect start in the third minute before Ezri Konsa doubled the lead 15 minutes later.

Bukayo Saka then took centre stage, scoring twice before halftime to leave England in complete control at the break.

France came out firing in the second half as Mbappé struck in the 48th and 66th minutes, with Bradley Barcola scoring in between to cut the deficit to just one goal.

Saka completed his hat-trick from the penalty spot in the 87th minute to make it 5-3, but Ousmane Dembélé pulled one back for France in stoppage time to set up a tense finish.

With France chasing an equaliser, Jude Bellingham struck in the eighth minute of added time to wrap up a pulsating 6-4 victory for England.

Meanwhile, in the FIFA World Cup final, Spain will take on Argentina at 7am tomorrow.

item.title: FRU CEO says no formal complaints lodged regarding Flying Fijians team environment
item.description: Fiji Rugby Union Chief Executive Officer Koli Sewabu says no formal reports or complaints have been brought to his attention regarding allegations of an unprofessional environment within the Flying Fijians camp. Sewabu made the comments in response to questions from fijivillage News following reports alleging the team environment has been unprofessional, with little planning and organisation, whil
item.content:encoded:
Fiji Rugby Union Chief Executive Officer Koli Sewabu says no formal reports or complaints have been brought to his attention regarding allegations of an unprofessional environment within the Flying Fijians camp.

Sewabu made the comments in response to questions from fijivillage News following reports alleging the team environment has been unprofessional, with little planning and organisation, while players were allegedly being treated more like children than elite athletes.

The reports also claimed inappropriate language and a communication style described by those close to the camp as unprofessional, condescending and unacceptable.

Responding to the allegations, Sewabu says Fiji Rugby has established governance and reporting processes to ensure any concerns relating to player welfare, team management or high-performance operations are raised, assessed and addressed through the appropriate channels.

He says no formal complaint or issue of the nature described has been brought to his attention through those established processes.

Sewabu says it would therefore be inappropriate to comment on or speculate about allegations that have not been formally presented or verified.

He says players, coaches and management all have a responsibility to uphold the values and standards expected of Fiji Rugby, and where genuine concerns exist, they should be raised through the appropriate mechanisms so they can be properly considered.

The FRU CEO says the union has full confidence in its governance structures to deal with any matters professionally, fairly and confidentially, adding that any formal concerns raised will receive the attention and due process they deserve.

As the team prepares to take on Scotland tomorrow morning, Sewabu says the immediate focus remains on the match, with preparations progressing well and the union looking forward to a strong performance.

He also thanked the Fijian community across the United Kingdom for their continued support, saying their encouragement has been a tremendous source of strength for the Flying Fijians.

The Flying Fijians will take on Scotland at 1.10am tomorrow.

In other matches today, the All Blacks will host Ireland at 7.10pm, Japan will take on France at 8.40pm, and the Wallabies will face Italy at 10.10pm.

South Africa will meet Wales at 3.40am, while England will take on Argentina at 7.10am tomorrow.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys top Group A after 3-0 win over PNG
item.description: The Junior Bula Boys have topped Group A at the OFC Under-16 Men's Championship after beating hosts Papua New Guinea 3-0. Fiji took a 3-0 lead into halftime and held on to the advantage until fulltime to secure all three points and finish at the top of the standings.
item.content:encoded:
The Junior Bula Boys have topped Group A at the OFC Under-16 Men's Championship after beating hosts Papua New Guinea 3-0.

Fiji took a 3-0 lead into halftime and held on to the advantage until fulltime to secure all three points and finish at the top of the standings.

Following the win, the team expressed their gratitude to their supporters and families back in Fiji for their continued support throughout the tournament.

The victory keeps Fiji's unbeaten run intact as they progress to the knockout stage of the competition.

The Junior Bula Boys will now face the Group B runners-up in the semi-finals on Wednesday at 4pm at PNG Football Stadium.

item.title: Bulldogs crush Tigers 32-0
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/07/2026 The Bulldogs demolished the Wests Tigers 32-0 in the NRL last night.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/07/2026

The Bulldogs demolished the Wests Tigers 32-0 in the NRL last night.

With Viliame Kikau back from a pectoral injury and Sean O'Sullivan starring in the halves, the Bulldogs racked up their eighth win of the season to climb to 11th on the ladder ahead of a blockbuster clash with the Warriors in Round 21.

20-year-old Jethro Rinakama scored the Bulldogs' first try, followed by Josh Curran and Enari Tuala, to give Canterbury an 18-0 halftime lead.

Matt Burton extended Canterbury's lead in the 65th minute, and Curran crossed for his second try in the 72nd minute to seal the victory.

Burton was flawless with the boot, converting all five tries and adding a penalty goal.

In other results, the Raiders defeated the Rabbitohs 32-24, while the Warriors beat the Dragons 20-12.

Looking at today's matches, the Titans will take on the Sea Eagles at 4pm, before the Dolphins meet the Cowboys at 6.05pm.

item.title: Lelean through to two Eastern Zone finals as QVS claims U14 spot
item.description: Lelean Memorial School secured places in two finals after recording two wins in the Vodafone Eastern Zone semi-finals rugby competition. In the Under-14 grade, Queen Victoria School defeated Lelean Memorial School 12-7 at Natovi Ground.
item.content:encoded:
Lelean Memorial School secured places in two finals after recording two wins in the Vodafone Eastern Zone semi-finals rugby competition.

In the Under-14 grade, Queen Victoria School defeated Lelean Memorial School 12-7 at Natovi Ground.

Lelean thrashed Tailevu North College 56-0 in the Under-15 grade at Dawasamu Ground.

In the Under-19 grade, Lelean also defeated Dawasamu Secondary School 15-3 .

In other matches, Ratu Kadavulevu School defeated Tailevu North College 42-0 in the Under-14 grade semi final at St Vincent Ground in Natovi.

RKS also edged Queen Victoria School 19-17 in the Under-15 grade semi final.

Meanwhile, Queen Victoria School defeated Sila Central College 25-11 in the Under-16 grade semi final.

item.title: Nasinu U18 to face Grammar in Southern Zone Deans final after semi-final wins
item.description: The Nasinu Secondary School Under 18 team will take on Suva Grammar High School the final of the Southern Zone Vodafone Deans final next week after they edged Marist Brothers High School 22-17 at the Grammar Grounds today . Grammar thumped Ratu Sukuna Memorial School 24-15 to book a spot in the final.
item.content:encoded:
The Nasinu Secondary School Under 18 team will take on Suva Grammar High School the final of the Southern Zone Vodafone Deans final next week after they edged Marist Brothers High School 22-17 at the Grammar Grounds today .

Grammar thumped Ratu Sukuna Memorial School 24-15 to book a spot in the final.

Grammar continued its dominance in the U17 grade semi final, defeating Naitasiri High School 34-7 while Gospel High School edged Marist15-10.

In the Weet Bix Raluve U16 grade, Grammar beat Lomaivuna Secondary School 15-7 to book a final spot next week.

The finals will be on Saturday at Grammar grounds.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys face hosts PNG in crucial OFC U-16 Championship clash
item.description: The Fiji Junior Bula Boys will face their biggest test of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship when they take on hosts Papua New Guinea today in their final group match. Fiji go into the match with confidence after two impressive victories, defeating New Caledonia 2-0 in their opening game before recording a dominant 5-0 win over Samoa.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Junior Bula Boys will face their biggest test of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship when they take on hosts Papua New Guinea today in their final group match.

Fiji go into the match with confidence after two impressive victories, defeating New Caledonia 2-0 in their opening game before recording a dominant 5-0 win over Samoa.

Head coach Sunil Kumar says Papua New Guinea will not be an easy opponent as they are the hosts and have shown they are a strong side.

He says Fiji must be more clinical in front of goal, and the players must convert their chances when they come.

The Junior Bula Boys will be aiming to continue their unbeaten run and finish the group stage strongly.

Fiji will face Papua New Guinea at 6pm at the PNG Football Stadium in Port Morseby.

The semi-finals of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship will be held next Thursday, while the final will be played next Saturday.

item.title: Labasa fights back twice to hold Suva in thrilling 2-all draw
item.description: Labasa fought back twice to earn a 2-all draw against Suva in an entertaining Extra Premier League clash.Suva got off to a flying

item.description: More than 51,000 fans are expected to fill Murrayfield Stadium tomorrow as the FIJI Water Flying Fijians prepare to face Scotland,

item.description: Marist Brothers High School began the Vodafone Southern Zone semi-finals on a high, beating Gospel High School 16-7 in the under-

item.title: Kiran rallies behind Fiji Women's Drua ahead of semi final showdown
item.description: The countdown is almost over as the McDonald's Fiji Women's Drua prepare to face the Queensland Reds in a blockbuster Super W semi

item.title: Kadavu defeat Skipper Cup champs Malolo in another warmup match
item.description: Kadavu Rugby continued its impressive build-up to next month's Skipper Cup with another stunning upset, defeating reigning champio
